title: Arguments in long RUN instructions should be sorted
description: Arguments in long RUN instructions should be sortedAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityCode SmellIn Dockerfiles, when commands within aRUNinstruction have a lot of arguments, especially those that install system packages, it is important to ensure that the arguments are sorted alphabetically (if the order is not enforced by a command). This practice enhances the readability and maintainability of the code. It allows for easier tracking of modifications and can help prevent potential errors.Why is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoWhen arguments inRUNinstructions are not sorted alphabetically, it can make the Dockerfile harder to read and maintain.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.description: This rule uses a custom function to check if arguments in long RUN instructions are sorted alphabetically. It focuses on package installation commands (apt-get, apk, yum, dnf) where multiple packages are listed. The function checks if package names are in alphabetical order to improve readability and maintainability.
